title: C Memory Outcomes, Evidence, and Toolchain Boundaries · Guided Pointer Studio
summary: A guided implementation pair receives a clean input trace and acceptance examples, then builds the first working memory-safety evidence and transfer matrix while narrating each size, lifetime, ownership, and cleanup decision. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of memory-safety evidence and transfer matrix while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate deterministic browser practice from C23 compiler, sanitizer, ABI, concurrency, fuzz, load, and production evidence.
Pin ISO/IEC 9899:2024, reviewed implementation support, diagnostics, extensions, and target ABI as a compatibility contract.
Trace source, preprocessing, translation, linking, loading, execution, and observable output without collapsing their failure layers.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Transfer Evidence Plan
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 8911. Produce bounded C evidence to separate browser evidence from native transfer evidence; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate deterministic browser practice from C23 compiler, sanitizer, ABI, concurrency, fuzz, load, and production evidence.

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Transfer Evidence Plan.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Evidence Limits
steps.instruction:
For this workshop demonstration of cmem-evidence-limits, write the complete compile-ready C program headed "/* Evidence: cmem-evidence-limits-ifn2tj */" for changed case 1260. It must separate browser evidence from native transfer evidence, reject one changed boundary, print an observable result and invariant, and return nonzero when the invariant fails. Keep instant code within the PicoC 3.2.2 practice subset; do not claim C23, optimizing compiler, sanitizer, ABI, thread, operating-system, FFI, load, or production evidence until its explicit controlled transfer gate passes.

For Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Evidence Limits, challenge the documented misconception with changed-size, failure-injection, printed invariant, compiler, sanitizer, analyzer, fuzz, ABI, concurrency, or production-transfer evidence: One clean sanitizer run proves absence of memory defects.

stimulus.title: Evidence Limits failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: One clean sanitizer run proves absence of memory defects.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State what tests, warnings, analyzers, sanitizers, proofs, reviews, and production telemetry can and cannot establish.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Evidence Limits condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Evidence Limits case, omit size, bounds, lifetime, owner, allocation failure, cleanup, invariant output, and native transfer gates, then call the browser result production-safe.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Evidence Limits
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Evidence Limits: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 6578. Produce bounded C evidence to separate browser evidence from native transfer evidence; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: State what tests, warnings, analyzers, sanitizers, proofs, reviews, and production telemetry can and cannot establish.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Evidence Limits.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Evidence Limits, keep Evidence Limits connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: State what tests, warnings, analyzers, sanitizers, proofs, reviews, and production telemetry can and cannot establish.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Evidence Limits, name the size, bounds, object, lifetime, owner, borrow, allocation, cleanup, representation, ABI, thread, and transfer boundary, Evidence Limits precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Workshop Evidence Limits, trace this concrete input and predict size arithmetic, pointer range, lifetime transition, allocation result, cleanup order, printed output, and native evidence still missing before reviewing it: A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 6578. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset.
